steps.title: Identify the structures around the blank
steps.content: A noun (method) is immediately followed by a participial phrase (called ELISA) that provides additional information about it.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A non-restrictive participial phrase is separated from its noun by a comma.

Option C correctly uses a comma between method and called ELISA; since the method is already identified from the previous sentence, this phrase is non-restrictive.

Option A creates nested or unbalanced parentheticals, option B uses mixed punctuation (em dash before, comma after), and option D lacks punctuation, treating the phrase as restrictive.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
